name: Hydrolysed collagen peptides
oneliner: Modest, replicated benefit on elasticity. Quality varies by brand.
eyebrow: Supplement · Hair & nails · Hydrolysed collagen peptides
headline: Hydrolysed collagen peptides
glance: Bovine type I/III · marine type I

evidence.note: Replicated improvement across 19+ trials. Effect size: small-to-moderate.
Modest TEWL reduction; clinically visible at 12 weeks.
Statistically significant, clinically subtle. Not a substitute for a retinoid.
Real signal but trial quality variable.

forms.note: Indian-market entry; dosing on the low end.
Marketing premium without formula advantage. Skip.

faq.a: Not directly — and any marketing that says so is wrong. The peptides are broken down to amino acids during digestion. The mechanism is plausibly upstream signalling (specifically dipeptides like Pro-Hyp) that stimulates fibroblast collagen synthesis. The result is real but modest, and takes 12 weeks to read on the face.

faq.q: Does the type matter (I, II, III, marine, bovine)?
faq.a: For skin endpoints, less than the marketing suggests. Most positive trials use type I (the dominant skin collagen) from either bovine or marine sources at 5–10 g/d. Type II is for cartilage research. Match price and dose, not type.

Not yet. 'Vegan collagen' powders contain amino acids that may support endogenous collagen synthesis but have no direct trial evidence for the same endpoints. Better to spend on diet protein + vitamin C and bank the remainder.
Either. Coffee is the most-popular vehicle and does not affect bioavailability.
